text.title: minimum-product-frameworks Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for minimum-product-frameworks as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Reflexively choosing MVP without checking competitor density, buyer type, or differentiator.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Just build an MVP" is a 2010s default that does not generalize to 2026 categories where AI-assisted competitors can clone functional surfaces in weeks. Framework choice must be grounded in market evidence.

failure-mode.description: Acronym soup as theatre: teams pick "MLP" because it sounds nicer than "MVP" without changing scope or adding exit criteria.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The framework name is irrelevant without a written exit criterion per framework. The choice is decorative unless it changes what gets built and when you stop.

failure-mode.description: Single-axis market read: using only blue/red ocean without considering buyer (B2B/consumer), distribution (PLG/sales-led), or regulatory load.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The nine-framework table under-specifies; in practice buyer type and distribution model often dominate the framework choice more than competitive density alone.

failure-mode.description: No transition rule: README says "MFP then expand" but doesn't define what triggers the transition (metric? time? validation event?).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents will sit in MFP forever without an explicit graduation trigger and acceptance criteria.

failure-mode.description: MAC ambiguity: "Minimum Awesome Component" overlaps heavily with MLP and SLC; in red oceans a MAC often is the lovable hook, double-counting the same scope.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The methodology doesn't fully disambiguate MAC from MLP; agents may conflate them without additional context about the category.

failure-mode.description: MVA vs MMP overlap: Both target enterprise/ROI; the methodology doesn't disambiguate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In practice, MMP = sellable, MVA = measurable business value — agents conflate them without clear distinction.

failure-mode.description: No cost dimension: all nine frameworks treated as equally expensive in the decision matrix.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: MLP and MDP cost 2-4x MVP for the polish budget; recommending MLP without surfacing the cost delta produces naive choices that surprise engineering in sprint planning.

failure-mode.description: Framework lock-in: Once "MLP" is in the deck, switching to RAT after a failed validation is socially expensive.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Methodology offers no de-escalation path. Re-picks should be reframed as learning, not failure.

failure-mode.description: Survivorship-bias source: "MVP is suicide in 2026" is a strong claim drawn from crowded-SaaS storytelling; doesn't generalise to vertical SaaS, B2B niches, or dev-tools where MVPs still win.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The framework is weighted toward horizontal SaaS assumptions; vertical and deep-tech contexts may validate differently.

failure-mode.description: Acronym hallucination: LLMs invent extra frameworks (MUP, MEP, MWP) under prompt pressure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Constrain to the exact 9-row enum; reject any output naming a framework outside the list.

failure-mode.description: Default-to-MVP drift: Any LLM trained on 2010s startup canon will pick MVP unless explicitly told to consider crowded-market evidence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force the agent to print the matrix score per framework before naming the winner.

failure-mode.description: Crowded-market over-claim: Without competitor evidence, agents will declare every market "crowded" to justify MLP.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require competitor_count + named competitors in market-context.yml; refuse to pick if missing.

failure-mode.description: Exit-criterion vagueness: Agents emit "good user feedback" or "high adoption" as exit criteria.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Validate that each criterion has a metric, a threshold, and a measurement window.

failure-mode.description: Framework-then-justify failure: Agent picks first, rationalises second.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mitigate by structured output: scores per framework BEFORE the chosen one, in a JSON schema the prompt enforces.

failure-mode.description: Confounding with mvp-scoping methodology: Agent jumps straight into in/out scope before picking the framework.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pipeline must enforce: choice file exists → only then scoping methodology runs.

failure-mode.description: Pivot inertia: Agent re-confirms the original framework even when metrics scream pivot.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a structured "stay vs pivot" decision with rejection-reason for the rejected option.

failure-mode.description: No human checkpoint on enterprise commitments: MMP/MVA implies sales contracts and SLAs — agents must not autonomously promote to these without a founder/PM sign-off step.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a human gate before MMP/MVA selection goes live.

failure-mode.description: Hand-off contract drift: Downstream methodology agents read the choice file; if framework changes mid-cycle without re-running the downstream agent, scope and framework drift apart.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CI: hash the choice file in the spec front-matter; mismatch fails the build.

failure-mode.description: 2026-claim staleness: README says "in 2026 MVP is suicide in crowded markets" — agents will quote this verbatim in pitches.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Phrase it as a heuristic, not a law, to avoid over-confident decks.
